query: How do I meet the Latin requirement — does school Latin count?

queries: How do I meet the Latin requirement — does school Latin count?

retrieved.title: Additional exams
retrieved.text: For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. You get all information about the additional exam and the regocnition of the ECTS for the minimum number of credits at the

retrieved.title: Additional exams
retrieved.text: can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. You get all information about the additional exam and the regocnition of the ECTS for the minimum number of credits at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/studium/zusatz-und-ergaenzungspruefungen/) (in German).

retrieved.title: Additional exams
retrieved.text: / - I've already passed the additional exam in Latin/Greek, but it still shows up in my student record sheet. What am I supposed to do? The completion of the additional exam in Latin or Greek should be registered automatically. If your school reports prove that you have sufficient knowledge, this is done directly during the first-time admission procedure. If you did not present the appropriate school reports at the Admission Office, you can send them via [contact form](/en/contact-form/). Please include your u:account e-mail address in your message. In case you take the additional exam at the University of Vienna, the [StudienServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at) of the corresponding course will register your grade. If this does not happen, please contact them directly. - I'm already studying at the University of Vienna. May I pass the additional exam which is required for a different degree programme at the University of Vienna? You are already studying at the University of Vienna and would like to start a second degree programme, for which Latin/Greek is required? In this case, you can pass the additional exam in Latin/Greek at the University of Vienna if you have already completed the STEOP of your current degree programme. The successful completion is a valid proof of the additional exam for the admission to another degree programme. - Where can I pass the additional exam outside of the University of

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text: subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - My English certificate is not in the list. Can it be accepted? No. Only the proofs and certificates mentioned above are accepted. - English is my first language, but I do not have any of the proofs mentioned above. Do I have to take an exam? If you do not have a proof of your language proficiency at level C1, the University of Vienna may prescribe an assessment by an institution chosen by the university in the course of the admission procedure. You have to pay any potentially occurring costs yourself. - I have a proof of English language proficiency at level C1. However, only a proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for my degree programme. Do I have to provide a separate proof? No. Upload your C1-proof in your application at "Further documents and proofs" as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2". - I have not yet received my secondary-school leaving certificate and want to apply for admission to the

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: time. If you have completed a course/exam multiple times, only the date of the last examination result counts. - I study at least one of my two teaching subjects at a university of art in Vienna. What are the requirements for my studies? If at least one subject in a teacher education combination is completed at a university of art in Vienna, the respective university of art is responsible for calculating the ECTS credits. The ECTS credits are calculated over the entire duration of the degree programme. Information about exams at the University of Vienna will be obtained if necessary. If students have completed less than 16 ECTS credits by the end of the fourth semester (deadline 31 October for the summer semester/31 March for the winter semester), it is up to the respective university of art to exclude the student from the degree programme after reviewing all information. ECTS credits obtained at the University of Vienna are displayed in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium). - Are there any programs that support me in my studies and in achieving the minimum number of credits? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/) Make use of the [services for students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) to achieve your goal. The

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text: / - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count as proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed a degree of at least two years, taught entirely in English, at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - I have a secondary-school leaving certificate from a non-EU/EEA country with the subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - Directorates of Education ("Bildungsdirektionen") The Directorate of Education is responsible for the schools in the respective austrian federal province. For some bachelor and diploma programmes, you have to provide proof of additional qualifications in addition to meeting the general admission requirements prior to admission (Latin, Greek, Descriptive Geometry). If you are unable to provide evidence of this knowledge via your school reports (school leaving certificate, annual reports of the individual school levels), you will have to make up for this externally. Please enquire about this at the Direcotrate of Education of the federal province in which you live. Related Links: - [Directorates of Education (only in German)](https://www.bmbwf.gv.at/Themen/schule/schulsystem/bdir/bildungsdirektionen.html) - Validating your u:card You have to validate your u:card at one of the u:card terminals at the beginning of every semester to guarantee its validity. You can validate it as soon as your tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee has the status “received on...” in u:space. Related Links: - [u:card – further information](https://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) - [Location of the u:card terminals](https://www.google.com/maps/d/viewer?mid=1TpC0t4Ukzm1dUPjyhW4SymBhCpI&ouid=0&ll=48.2113223635795%2C16.35953165&z=14) Related entries: - [u:card (student ID)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31990&cHash=9f523509641ed0e36c663dab79364d34) - Vienna University Computer Center (Zentraler Informatikdienst, ZID) The task of the Vienna University Computer Center (ZID) is to support the University of Vienna with a stable and modern IT infrastructure and a wide range of IT services. The Vienna University Computer Center (ZID) develops, implements and maintains over 400 IT services at the University of

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: your teaching subjects with the subject of your complementary degree programme. You have to obtain the minimum number of credits within the remaining fourth semester of the combination programme. Exams completed in the complementary degree programme count, if you completed them within the first four semesters of the combination programme and if you apply for recognition in due time (see FAQ: “Are recognised achievements accepted for the minimum number of credits?”). - Are recognised achievements accepted for the minimum number of credits? Whether a recognised achievement is accepted for the minimum number of credits depends on the date on which the achievement (= initial achievement) was completed. The initial achievement must have been completed within the period relevant for the minimum number of credits from the first start of a degree programme and its recognition must have been approved. Only then does the recognised achievement count towards the minimum number of credits. Therefore, apply for recognition at your responsible StudiesServiceCenter well in advance. Note that it might take up to two months until a decision is taken and that the complaints procedure in case of a negative decision also takes time. If you have completed a course/exam multiple times, only the date of the last examination result counts. - I study at least one of my two teaching subjects at a university of art in Vienna. What are the requirements

retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text: for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively, you can also use the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass) which outlines the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which achievements you have already completed and which ones you have to complete next. In the [course directory u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/index.html), you can select the specific courses for the current semester. Factors, such as content, time, venue, lecturer, etc., may help you in your decision. The workload required to complete a course is indicated in the form of ECTS credits. On average, completing a degree programme within its standard duration takes 30 ECTS credits per semester. Please note that you have to complete an [introductory and orientation period (STEOP)](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/) at the beginning of every bachelor’s programme before you are allowed to register for any other courses. In addition to compulsory and elective modules, you will have to complete [extension curricula](/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/) (EC) in almost all bachelor’s programmes. You can register for EC in u:space. Tips for semester planning in u:find The course directory u:find offers three help tools for semester planning, which are still known to only a few students: - In the overview of all courses in

retrieved.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
retrieved.text: ## / - Step 1: Check your record of examinations and/or semester planner You can find the record of examinations in u:space under the tab “Studies”. It is organised according to the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which courses you have already completed, which achievements you have to complete next and what requirements you have to fulfil for them. You can find the semester planner in u:space under the tab “Courses”. It shows you the courses currently offered and provides you with a calendar to help you plan your semester. Attention: Please always check the [curriculum of your degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula). The record of examinations in u:space supports you in planning, but you have to select the correct curriculum point yourself on the basis of your curriculum when registering for the course or exam - Step 2: Select an achievement from your curriculum Search for modules for which you still have to complete courses (red and orange traffic light symbol) in your record of examinations in u:space. After that with the help of the record of examinations and by looking at your [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula), determine for which curriculum point (module) you are still missing a course / exam. To find out which courses you can attend for the curriculum point you have not yet completed, either click on "Link to u:find" at the book icon

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
heading_path: Recognised proofs of English language proficiency at level B2
retrieved.text:
- Secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English. The following conditions apply:
  - The secondary-school leaving certificate was completed at a school of an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
  - If the level of English proficiency is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded.
  - The Austrian secondary-school leaving certificate (Matura) of the Corona year 2021 is equal to other secondary-school leaving certificates.
- "IB diploma" according to the regulations of the "International Baccalaureate Organization" with the subject English.
- Certificate of the supplementary examination in the subject English from the University Preparation Programme of an Austrian University.
- "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" from an Austrian university with a successfully completed examination in the subject English.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement, which fulfills all of the following conditions:
  - full-time degree programme of at least two years and
  - taught entirely in English and
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

Any of the above mentioned proofs at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

retrieved.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
retrieved.text:
You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you fulfil the following:

- You work as teacher at a primary or secondary school with publics rights within the EU/EEA or Switzerland.

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Send the following proofs via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)
   1. all pages of your secondary-school leaving certificate/degree certificate merged into one PDF file
      including translation if the document was not issued in German or English
   2. a confirmation about your work issued by the headmaster
3. State which subjects you want to study in your message.

retrieved.text: and exams via u:find resp. the record of examinations in u:space. In order to be able to attend courses with continuous assessment or exams, you have to register in due time. You can find the registration periods within the respective courses in u:find. In order to register successfully, you have to fulfil the requirements according to the curriculum of your degree programme. This is being evaluated in the registration process. It is not possible to attend a course with continuous assesment if you have not registered in due time. The registration for courses with non-continuous assessment is not mandatory but recommended. You cannot be registered after the registration/late registration period has ended. If you have registered correctly, you are entitled to attend the course/exam. retrieved.text: applies to citizens of other countries than Türkiye) with [Apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828). --- Exception: If you have already completed a bachelor/diploma programme (at least three years duration or at least 180 ECTS) you do not need a proof of your university entrance exam. The further procedure depends which country you completed your previous studies in. - Ukraine Starting from the beginning of the application period for the summer semester 2027: If you have a Ukrainan secondary-school leaving certificate and are applying for admission to a bachelor’s or diploma programme, you will need the NMT (National Multi-Subject Test): - a minimum of 100 points required - validity: 4 years - applies to diplomas awarded starting in the 2021–2022 school year - must be completed in at least 4 subjects In addition, your Ukrainian educational documents require an [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828). - United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland ("A-levels") If you receive the General Certificate of Education (GCE/"A-Levels"), you have to fulfil the following requirements: - Four general subjects that are independent from one another have to be completed with grade A\*, A, B or C (respectively grades 9 - 4). These subjects must include: - a foreign language - Mathematics - one of the subjects Biology, Physics or Chemistry - a subject of the Humanities - At least three of the four subjects have to be completed at the GCE Advanced level, the

retrieved.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Creative teaching subject at a university of arts
retrieved.text: You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you study at least one teaching subject at one of the following universities of arts - [University of Music and Performing Arts Vienna](https://www.mdw.ac.at/studiencenter/zulassung-zum-universitaetsuebergreifenden-lehramts-studium/) (only available in German) - [Universität of Applied Arts Vienna](https://www.dieangewandte.at/lehramt_kombination) (only available in German) - [Akademie der bildenden Künste Wien](https://www.akbild.ac.at/de/studium/zulassungspruefungen/bachelorstudium-kuenstlerisches-lehramt) (only available in German) You first participate in the aptitude test procedure of the university of arts. After you were successful you can be admitted to one or more teaching subjects at the University of Vienna. If you want to study all teaching subjects at the university of arts you will attend the general educational basics at the University of Vienna as part of a [co-registration](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/co-registration/). #### Follow these steps: Collapse all Open all ##### Step 1: Register in u:space You already have a u:account, but it expired? Reactivate your u:account via the [website of the Vienna University Computer Center](https://account.forms.univie.ac.at/uaccount-reaktivieren/index.html?lang=en). You don't have a u:account? 1. Create a u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/) within the admission period. 2. Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. 3. Log into u:space and complete the registration. ##### Step 2: Complete the admission at the university of arts Complete the admission procedure at the university of arts. retrieved.title: Test details: Test literature and sample exercises
retrieved.text:
Collapse all

Open all

#### Test literature

[Aufnahmeverfahren für das Diplomstudium der Rechtswissenschaften an der Universität Wien – Auszug basierend auf „Grundbegriffe der Rechtswissenschaften“ von Franz-Stefan Meissel; Helmut Ofner; Bettina Perthold-Stoitzner; Michaela Windisch-Graetz](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:2046181) (only available in German)

#### Sample exercises and test structure

[Sample exercises and test structure for the diploma programme in Law](https://self-assessment2.pzle.univie.ac.at/start.php?id=preav_rewi) (only available in German)

Collapse all

Open all

#### Ranking

The ranking is determined as follows:

- 400 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with German as their A-language
- 262 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with a different language as their A-language

After these, participants with descending overall scores follow alternately (according to the zipper merge method) on the ranking list:

- one test participant who registered with a language other than German as A-language,
- and one test participant who registered with German as A-language.

To complete the admission procedure you also have to fulfil the requirements for admission to a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

#### Test literature

[Transkulturelle Kommunikation: Zur Einführung und Vorbereitung auf das Studium](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:1165618) (only available in German)

#### Sample exercises and test structure

[Sample exercises and test structure for the bachelor's programme in Transcultural Communication](https://self-assessment2.pzle.univie.ac.at/start.php?id=preav_trans) (only available in German)

How should I proceed? Upload the final transcript of your previous school year as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2" at "Further documents and proofs" in your application for admission in u:space. retrieved.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Active/completed programme in Teacher Education at another educational institution
retrieved.text:
You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you have studied or are currently studying one of the following programmes at a university or college of teacher education in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland.

Sie sind vom Eignungsverfahren für das Bachelorstudium Lehramt an der Universität Wien ausgenommen, wenn Sie eines der folgenden Studien an einer Universität oder Pädagogischen Hochschule eines EU/EWR-Landes oder der Schweiz studieren oder abgeschlossen haben.

- Bachelor's programme in Teacher Education for secondary schools (general education)
- Programme in Business Education
- Programme in Religious Education

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Send the following proofs via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)
   1. all pages of your secondary-school leaving certificate merged into one PDF file
      including translation if the document was not issued in German or English
   2. your current confirmation of studies or your degree certificate
3. State which subjects you want to study in your message. Note: If you want to study the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education please also send your confirmation of studies to [StudiesServiceCenter Sport Science](#).

reranked.text: / - I've already passed the additional exam in Latin/Greek, but it still shows up in my student record sheet. What am I supposed to do? The completion of the additional exam in Latin or Greek should be registered automatically. If your school reports prove that you have sufficient knowledge, this is done directly during the first-time admission procedure. If you did not present the appropriate school reports at the Admission Office, you can send them via [contact form](/en/contact-form/). Please include your u:account e-mail address in your message. In case you take the additional exam at the University of Vienna, the [StudienServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at) of the corresponding course will register your grade. If this does not happen, please contact them directly. - I'm already studying at the University of Vienna. May I pass the additional exam which is required for a different degree programme at the University of Vienna? You are already studying at the University of Vienna and would like to start a second degree programme, for which Latin/Greek is required? In this case, you can pass the additional exam in Latin/Greek at the University of Vienna if you have already completed the STEOP of your current degree programme. The successful completion is a valid proof of the additional exam for the admission to another degree programme. - Where can I pass the additional exam outside of the University of

Can it be accepted? No. Only the proofs and certificates mentioned above are accepted. - English is my first language, but I do not have any of the proofs mentioned above. Do I have to take an exam? If you do not have a proof of your language proficiency at level C1, the University of Vienna may prescribe an assessment by an institution chosen by the university in the course of the admission procedure. You have to pay any potentially occurring costs yourself. - I have a proof of English language proficiency at level C1. However, only a proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for my degree programme. Do I have to provide a separate proof? reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: time. If you have completed a course/exam multiple times, only the date of the last examination result counts. - I study at least one of my two teaching subjects at a university of art in Vienna. What are the requirements for my studies? If at least one subject in a teacher education combination is completed at a university of art in Vienna, the respective university of art is responsible for calculating the ECTS credits. The ECTS credits are calculated over the entire duration of the degree programme. Information about exams at the University of Vienna will be obtained if necessary. If students have completed less than 16 ECTS credits by the end of the fourth semester (deadline 31 October for the summer semester/31 March for the winter semester), it is up to the respective university of art to exclude the student from the degree programme after reviewing all information. ECTS credits obtained at the University of Vienna are displayed in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium). - Are there any programs that support me in my studies and in achieving the minimum number of credits? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/) Make use of the [services for students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) to achieve your goal. The

reranked.title: Proof of English language proficiency
reranked.text: / - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count as proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed a degree of at least two years, taught entirely in English, at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - I have a secondary-school leaving certificate from a non-EU/EEA country with the subject English. You have to obtain the minimum number of credits within the remaining fourth semester of the combination programme. Exams completed in the complementary degree programme count, if you completed them within the first four semesters of the combination programme and if you apply for recognition in due time (see FAQ: “Are recognised achievements accepted for the minimum number of credits?”). - Are recognised achievements accepted for the minimum number of credits? Whether a recognised achievement is accepted for the minimum number of credits depends on the date on which the achievement (= initial achievement) was completed. The initial achievement must have been completed within the period relevant for the minimum number of credits from the first start of a degree programme and its recognition must have been approved. Only then does the recognised achievement count towards the minimum number of credits. Therefore, apply for recognition at your responsible StudiesServiceCenter well in advance. Note that it might take up to two months until a decision is taken and that the complaints procedure in case of a negative decision also takes time. If you have completed a course/exam multiple times, only the date of the last examination result counts. - I study at least one of my two teaching subjects at a university of art in Vienna. reranked.title: Planning your semester
reranked.text: for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively, you can also use the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass) which outlines the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which achievements you have already completed and which ones you have to complete next. In the [course directory u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/index.html), you can select the specific courses for the current semester. Factors, such as content, time, venue, lecturer, etc., may help you in your decision. The workload required to complete a course is indicated in the form of ECTS credits. On average, completing a degree programme within its standard duration takes 30 ECTS credits per semester. Please note that you have to complete an [introductory and orientation period (STEOP)](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/) at the beginning of every bachelor’s programme before you are allowed to register for any other courses. In addition to compulsory and elective modules, you will have to complete [extension curricula](/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/) (EC) in almost all bachelor’s programmes. You can register for EC in u:space. Tips for semester planning in u:find The course directory u:find offers three help tools for semester planning, which are still known to only a few students: - In the overview of all courses in

reranked.title: Proof of English language proficiency
heading_path: Recognised proofs of English language proficiency at level B2
reranked.text:
- Secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English. The following conditions apply:
  - The secondary-school leaving certificate was completed at a school of an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
  - If the level of English proficiency is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded.
  - The Austrian secondary-school leaving certificate (Matura) of the Corona year 2021 is equal to other secondary-school leaving certificates.
- "IB diploma" according to the regulations of the "International Baccalaureate Organization" with the subject English.
- Certificate of the supplementary examination in the subject English from the University Preparation Programme of an Austrian University.
- "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" from an Austrian university with a successfully completed examination in the subject English.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement, which fulfills all of the following conditions:
  - full-time degree programme of at least two years and
  - taught entirely in English and
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

Any of the above mentioned proofs at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

reranked.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
reranked.text:
You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you fulfil the following:

- You work as teacher at a primary or secondary school with publics rights within the EU/EEA or Switzerland.

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Send the following proofs via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)
   1. all pages of your secondary-school leaving certificate/degree certificate merged into one PDF file
      including translation if the document was not issued in German or English
   2. a confirmation about your work issued by the headmaster
3. State which subjects you want to study in your message.

It is not possible to attend a course with continuous assesment if you have not registered in due time. The registration for courses with non-continuous assessment is not mandatory but recommended. The further procedure depends which country you completed your previous studies in. - Ukraine Starting from the beginning of the application period for the summer semester 2027: If you have a Ukrainan secondary-school leaving certificate and are applying for admission to a bachelor’s or diploma programme, you will need the NMT (National Multi-Subject Test): - a minimum of 100 points required - validity: 4 years - applies to diplomas awarded starting in the 2021–2022 school year - must be completed in at least 4 subjects In addition, your Ukrainian educational documents require an [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828). - United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland ("A-levels") If you receive the General Certificate of Education (GCE/"A-Levels"), you have to fulfil the following requirements: - Four general subjects that are independent from one another have to be completed with grade A\*, A, B or C (respectively grades 9 - 4). These subjects must include: - a foreign language - Mathematics - one of the subjects Biology, Physics or Chemistry - a subject of the Humanities - At least three of the four subjects have to be completed at the GCE Advanced level, the

heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education

reranked.text: You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you study at least one teaching subject at one of the following universities of arts - [University of Music and Performing Arts Vienna](https://www.mdw.ac.at/studiencenter/zulassung-zum-universitaetsuebergreifenden-lehramts-studium/) (only available in German) - [Universität of Applied Arts Vienna](https://www.dieangewandte.at/lehramt_kombination) (only available in German) - [Akademie der bildenden Künste Wien](https://www.akbild.ac.at/de/studium/zulassungspruefungen/bachelorstudium-kuenstlerisches-lehramt) (only available in German) You first participate in the aptitude test procedure of the university of arts. After you were successful you can be admitted to one or more teaching subjects at the University of Vienna. reranked.title: Test details: Test literature and sample exercises
reranked.text:
Collapse all

Open all

#### Test literature

[Aufnahmeverfahren für das Diplomstudium der Rechtswissenschaften an der Universität Wien – Auszug basierend auf „Grundbegriffe der Rechtswissenschaften“ von Franz-Stefan Meissel; Helmut Ofner; Bettina Perthold-Stoitzner; Michaela Windisch-Graetz](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:2046181) (only available in German)

#### Sample exercises and test structure

[Sample exercises and test structure for the diploma programme in Law](https://self-assessment2.pzle.univie.ac.at/start.php?id=preav_rewi) (only available in German)

Collapse all

Open all

#### Ranking

The ranking is determined as follows:

- 400 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with German as their A-language
- 262 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with a different language as their A-language

After these, participants with descending overall scores follow alternately (according to the zipper merge method) on the ranking list:

- one test participant who registered with a language other than German as A-language,
- and one test participant who registered with German as A-language.

To complete the admission procedure you also have to fulfil the requirements for admission to a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

#### Test literature

[Transkulturelle Kommunikation: Zur Einführung und Vorbereitung auf das Studium](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:1165618) (only available in German)

#### Sample exercises and test structure

[Sample exercises and test structure for the bachelor's programme in Transcultural Communication](https://self-assessment2.pzle.univie.ac.at/start.php?id=preav_trans) (only available in German)

How should I proceed? Upload the final transcript of your previous school year as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2" at "Further documents and proofs" in your application for admission in u:space. You have to upload the secondary-school leaving certificate with the exam in the subject English before you complete the admission procedure.

reranked.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Active/completed programme in Teacher Education at another educational institution
reranked.text:
You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you have studied or are currently studying one of the following programmes at a university or college of teacher education in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland.

Sie sind vom Eignungsverfahren für das Bachelorstudium Lehramt an der Universität Wien ausgenommen, wenn Sie eines der folgenden Studien an einer Universität oder Pädagogischen Hochschule eines EU/EWR-Landes oder der Schweiz studieren oder abgeschlossen haben.

- Bachelor's programme in Teacher Education for secondary schools (general education)
- Programme in Business Education
- Programme in Religious Education

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Send the following proofs via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)
   1. all pages of your secondary-school leaving certificate merged into one PDF file
      including translation if the document was not issued in German or English
   2. your current confirmation of studies or your degree certificate
3. State which subjects you want to study in your message. Note: If you want to study the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education please also send your confirmation of studies to [StudiesServiceCenter Sport Science](#).

selected.title: Additional exams
selected.text: For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. You get all information about the additional exam and the regocnition of the ECTS for the minimum number of credits at the

selected.text: subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - My English certificate is not in the list. Can it be accepted? No. Only the proofs and certificates mentioned above are accepted. - English is my first language, but I do not have any of the proofs mentioned above. Do I have to take an exam? If you do not have a proof of your language proficiency at level C1, the University of Vienna may prescribe an assessment by an institution chosen by the university in the course of the admission procedure. You have to pay any potentially occurring costs yourself. - I have a proof of English language proficiency at level C1. However, only a proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for my degree programme. Do I have to provide a separate proof? No. Upload your C1-proof in your application at "Further documents and proofs" as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2". - I have not yet received my secondary-school leaving certificate and want to apply for admission to the

selected.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
selected.text: time. If you have completed a course/exam multiple times, only the date of the last examination result counts. - I study at least one of my two teaching subjects at a university of art in Vienna. What are the requirements for my studies? If at least one subject in a teacher education combination is completed at a university of art in Vienna, the respective university of art is responsible for calculating the ECTS credits. The ECTS credits are calculated over the entire duration of the degree programme. Information about exams at the University of Vienna will be obtained if necessary. If students have completed less than 16 ECTS credits by the end of the fourth semester (deadline 31 October for the summer semester/31 March for the winter semester), it is up to the respective university of art to exclude the student from the degree programme after reviewing all information. ECTS credits obtained at the University of Vienna are displayed in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium). - Are there any programs that support me in my studies and in achieving the minimum number of credits? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/) Make use of the [services for students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) to achieve your goal. The

selected.title: Proof of English language proficiency
selected.text: / - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count as proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed a degree of at least two years, taught entirely in English, at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - I have a secondary-school leaving certificate from a non-EU/EEA country with the subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom

assemble.context:
[1] Additional exams — Latin
For some degree programmes, an additional qualification in Latin has to be attained to gain admission. If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission. - Prior to admission: For some degree programmes, you have to prove your knowledge prior to admission. If you did not attend enough classes of Latin at school, you have to prove your knowledge by other means. Contact the [Bildungsdirektion Wien](https://www.bildung-wien.gv.at/schulen/Externistenangelegenheiten.html) (in German) about your options. - During the degree programme: For some degree programmes, it is sufficient to pass the additional exam in Latin during the degree programme (in the case of diploma programmes during the first stage of the degree programme). The additional exam may not be the last exam of your degree programme (or first stage in case of diploma programmes). If you do not have the required proof, the need to pass the additional exam will be specified during the admission procedure. This will be stated in your student record sheet. The additional exam can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. You get all information about the additional exam and the regocnition of the ECTS for the minimum number of credits at the

[2] Additional exams — Latin
can be passed by using the offered courses of the Department for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin. You get all information about the additional exam and the regocnition of the ECTS for the minimum number of credits at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) Philological and Cultural Studies](https://ssc-philkultur.univie.ac.at/studium/zusatz-und-ergaenzungspruefungen/) (in German).

[3] Additional exams — FAQ
/ - I've already passed the additional exam in Latin/Greek, but it still shows up in my student record sheet. What am I supposed to do? The completion of the additional exam in Latin or Greek should be registered automatically. If your school reports prove that you have sufficient knowledge, this is done directly during the first-time admission procedure. If you did not present the appropriate school reports at the Admission Office, you can send them via [contact form](/en/contact-form/). Please include your u:account e-mail address in your message. In case you take the additional exam at the University of Vienna, the [StudienServiceCenter (SSC)](http://ssc.univie.ac.at) of the corresponding course will register your grade. If this does not happen, please contact them directly. - I'm already studying at the University of Vienna. May I pass the additional exam which is required for a different degree programme at the University of Vienna? You are already studying at the University of Vienna and would like to start a second degree programme, for which Latin/Greek is required? In this case, you can pass the additional exam in Latin/Greek at the University of Vienna if you have already completed the STEOP of your current degree programme. The successful completion is a valid proof of the additional exam for the admission to another degree programme. - Where can I pass the additional exam outside of the University of

[4] Proof of English language proficiency — FAQ
subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - My English certificate is not in the list. Can it be accepted? No. Only the proofs and certificates mentioned above are accepted. - English is my first language, but I do not have any of the proofs mentioned above. Do I have to take an exam? If you do not have a proof of your language proficiency at level C1, the University of Vienna may prescribe an assessment by an institution chosen by the university in the course of the admission procedure. You have to pay any potentially occurring costs yourself. - I have a proof of English language proficiency at level C1. However, only a proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for my degree programme. Do I have to provide a separate proof? No. Upload your C1-proof in your application at "Further documents and proofs" as "Certificate of proficiency in English at level B2". - I have not yet received my secondary-school leaving certificate and want to apply for admission to the

The ECTS credits are calculated over the entire duration of the degree programme. Information about exams at the University of Vienna will be obtained if necessary. If students have completed less than 16 ECTS credits by the end of the fourth semester (deadline 31 October for the summer semester/31 March for the winter semester), it is up to the respective university of art to exclude the student from the degree programme after reviewing all information. ECTS credits obtained at the University of Vienna are displayed in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium). - Are there any programs that support me in my studies and in achieving the minimum number of credits? [6] Proof of English language proficiency — FAQ
/ - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count as proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed a degree of at least two years, taught entirely in English, at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - I have a secondary-school leaving certificate from a non-EU/EEA country with the subject English. prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Question: How do I meet the Latin requirement — does school Latin count?

generate.raw_response: If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given [1]. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission [1].

answer: If you attended Latin at a secondary school for at least ten hours per week, the knowledge is taken as given [1]. You have to prove this by means of your school transcripts during the first-time admission [1].
